evals.prompt: Two versions of a skill produced identical, correct outputs on every task. Version A ran on sonnet, version B on haiku, and both used roughly equal token counts. Which version should be adopted, and why?
evals.expected_output: Adopt the cheaper version (B / haiku): quality is at parity, and price-weighted cost favors haiku (~3.3x cheaper) even though raw token counts are equal. Raw token count is the wrong cost measure across models.
expectations: Justifies cost by PRICE-WEIGHTING per model, not by raw token count
Notes quality parity (equal correctness) as the precondition for down-tiering

evals.prompt: An A/B comparison ran only 1 repetition on 1 eval and version B looks slightly better. What verdict should be returned?
evals.expected_output: INCONCLUSIVE / underpowered: do not declare a winner from 1 rep on 1 eval; recommend increasing repetitions and adding evals before deciding.
expectations: Returns INCONCLUSIVE (or explicitly refuses to declare a winner)
Cites low statistical power (too few reps/evals) as the reason

evals.prompt: How do I A/B test the beta version of the right-size-skills skill against the stable one?
evals.expected_output: Use the --channels mode: it auto-locates the installed stable and beta copies and compares them, running each on its own model and returning a cost-adjusted verdict. Requires both the stable and beta marketplaces to be added.
expectations: Points to the --channels mode (or resolve_channels.py) to locate the two installed copies
Notes that both stable and beta marketplaces must be added first
Ends in a cost-adjusted verdict rather than a static assessment
